---
number-sections: false
---

<!-- Title as an .unnumbered H1, not a YAML `title:` -- these are standalone
     reference pages reached from the site header, not numbered chapters.
     Note: do NOT add a YAML `subtitle:` here. With a subtitle but no title,
     Quarto fails to infer a nav label and renders an EMPTY sidebar link. -->

# Data & Code {.unnumbered}

*The reproducibility layer.*

Every package this book leans on, grouped by what it does, coloured by
language, and wired back to the chapters that call it. Drag a node to
rearrange; hover a package for what it's used for.

```{=html}
<div class="hm-pkg-legend" style="margin-bottom:14px;">
  <div class="item"><span class="dot r"></span>R</div>
  <div class="item"><span class="dot python"></span>Python</div>
  <div class="item"><span class="dot chapter"></span>chapter</div>
</div>

<div class="hm-pkg-canvas column-page" data-hm-packages></div>
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
The graph is built from `assets/hail-mary-data.js`, which lists the packages
actually loaded across `notebooks/`, `scripts/` and `shiny/`. It is maintained
by hand — if you add or drop a dependency, update that file so the map stays
honest. The authoritative, machine-checkable records are `renv.lock` (exact
pinned versions for the whole compendium) and
`packages/potytools/DESCRIPTION` (the package's own declared dependencies).
:::

## Reproducing the analysis

All computationally intensive steps — NCBI retrieval, MAFFT alignment, IQ-TREE
inference — are orchestrated by a `targets` pipeline, so nothing here depends
on remembering which script to run in which order.

```r
# Install the pipeline dependencies once
install.packages(c("targets", "tarchetypes"))
remotes::install_local("packages/potytools")

renv::restore()     # exact package versions used for the rendered book

targets::tar_make()        # runs the pipeline, skipping up-to-date steps
targets::tar_visnetwork()  # inspect the dependency graph
```

External tools required on `PATH` for a full pipeline run: `mafft`, `iqtree2`,
`hmmscan`.

Not everything flows through the pipeline: the ELM short-linear-motif search
runs via a separate `gget`-based path
(`scripts/prep_elm_regions.R` → `scripts/gget_elm_run.py`), described in
[Chapter 4](notebooks/04_protein_motifs.qmd).

## potytools

The reusable analysis functions — GenBank parsing, codon usage bias
(RSCU/ENC/CAI/GC), host mono/dicot classification, motif-flank extraction,
phylogenetics helpers, sequence logos — live in an installable, tested R
package rather than being redefined chapter by chapter.

```r
remotes::install_local("packages/potytools")
library(potytools)
```

It targets rOpenSci standards: documented with roxygen2, tested with testthat,
lint-clean and `R CMD check`-clean, and is being prepared for peer review.

## Where the data lives

`data/` and `embeds/` are **not** tracked in git — they're large, regenerable,
or carry third-party licensing that isn't ours to redistribute. Regenerate them
with the pipeline above. Provenance for every external input is documented in
the [data sources appendix](notebooks/A1_data_sources.qmd).

```{=html}
<div class="hm-downloads" style="margin-top:2rem;">
  <a class="hm-btn accent" href="https://github.com/MintyHybrid/project-hail-mary" rel="noopener">↗ view the repository on GitHub</a>
  <a class="hm-btn" href="https://github.com/MintyHybrid/project-hail-mary/archive/refs/heads/main.zip" rel="noopener">⭳ download the code (.zip)</a>
</div>
```

## Package citations

If you use this work, please cite the underlying packages as well as the book.
In R, `citation("packagename")` gives the canonical reference for any of them;
the full set used here is collected in `packages.bib`.
